programme.desc: JB Gill reads Ah-Choo! by Suzanne Lang, a story of a brother and sister who are trying to find the perfect family pet(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: The Good Dinosaur (2015)
programme.desc: In a world where the asteroid impact that wiped out the dinosaurs never happened, humans have evolved as a primitive species while the giant lizards remain the dominant life form on Earth. A young apatosaurus becomes separated from his family, but finds a friend in a feral human boy as he makes the dangerous journey home. Pixar animated adventure, with the voices of Raymond Ochoa and Jack Bright(n)

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Snow
programme.desc: Undercover cameras reveal how living in the snow tests animals to their limit. In New Zealand, cameras follow a group of kea parrots, revealing how friendship is at the heart of the lives of these sociable and highly intelligent birds. In the North Pole, cameras get up close to play-fighting polar bears, while on the other side of the planet at the South Pole, footage reveals the parental devotion that emperor penguins have as they try to secure the survival of their offspring and face some of the harshest conditions on Earth. In Tasmania, the spy wallaby finds love - discovering along the way an interesting courtship technique among wombats(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Room on the Broom
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It tells the story of a kind witch who invites a surprising number of animals to ride on her broom - much to the frustration of her cat. But when a fearsome dragon appears, what will the gang do? Gillian Anderson heads the voice cast as the witch, with Rob Brydon, Martin Clunes, Sally Hawkins and David Walliams as the animals, Timothy Spall as the dragon and Simon Pegg as narrator(n)

programme.title: The Boss Baby (2017)
programme.desc: A youngster believes his baby brother is actually a power-hungry tycoon sent to destroy his perfect family life. However, the duo must put aside their differences and work together when they uncover a plot to sabotage the company their parents work for. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Alec Baldwin, Steve Buscemi, Jimmy Kimmel and Lisa Kudrow(n)

programme.title: Mimi and the Mountain Dragon
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Michael Morpurgo's children's book about a brave but shy girl from a village that lives in constant fear of a terrible mountain dragon. On a snowy winter's night, she finds a baby dragon asleep in her family's woodshed, and sets out on a terrible journey through a blizzard to return the infant to its dreaded mother. Featuring an introduction by the author and a score by Oscar-winning composer Rachel Portman, performed by the BBC Philharmonic(n)

programme.title: The Jungle Book (2016)
programme.desc: A boy is raised by animals in the jungles of India, but his carefree life is threatened by the return of a fierce tiger with a grudge against humans. The youngster faces a difficult choice between remaining with his friends or returning to his own people. Live action remake of Disney's animated adventure based on Rudyard Kipling's stories, starring Neel Sethi, with the voices of Bill Murray, Ben Kingsley and Idris Elba(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: City children Susan and John are spending their summer at Scatterbrook Farm, where they are amazed to discover the scarecrow of Ten Acre Field can come to life. But it seems this extraordinary new friendship has thrown the rhythm of the natural world out of kilter and now they must work to put things right. Mackenzie Crook writes, directs and stars in the first of two new dramas based on the books by Barbara Euphan Todd - last adapted 40 years ago in the popular ITV series with Jon Pertwee. Co-starring Steve Pemberton, Rosie Cavaliero and Vicki Pepperdine(n)

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with the voices of Ben Whishaw and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The events of Christmas Day weigh heavy in Walford, with more than one family left ripped apart. Sharon and Keanu's worlds have been shattered - but how far has Phil gone? On a happier note, Gray and Chantelle are excited about renewing their wedding vows but their plans are ruined when the venue cancels. Patrick is unhappy about Denise meddling in his personal life and takes an extreme course of action(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure victory. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the Christmas fun are Liz Bonnin, Stephen Merchant, Sharon Osbourne and Ranj Singh(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker introduces highlights of the Boxing Day Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool at the King Power Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Foxes and Reds had both made excellent starts to the season, with Brendan Rodgers' side emerging as surprise contenders for the title - or at the very least a Champions League place. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Norwich City at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Arsenal at the Vitality Stadium, Chelsea v Southampton at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v West Ham United at Selhurst Park, Everton v Burnley at Goodison Park, Manchester United v Newcastle United at Old Trafford, and Sheffield United v Watford at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The comedy star brings the latest series of his entertainment showcase to a conclusion with his annual festive special from the London Palladium. Tonight, global superstar Sharon Osbourne hands over her phone to play Send to All?, and Strictly Come Dancing judge Craig Revel Horwood is woken up by Michael in the middle of the night for the Midnight Gameshow. Plus, another Unexpected Star puts on a spectacular festive performance(n)

programme.title: Take Off with Bradley and Holly
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh and Holly Willoughby present a special Christmas family entertainment show, which offers members of the audience the chance to win the holiday of a lifetime to Lapland. The show will also feature special games, surprises and appearances by celebrity guests(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker introduces highlights of the Boxing Day Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool at the King Power Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Foxes and Reds had both made excellent starts to the season, with Brendan Rodgers' side emerging as surprise contenders for the title - or at the very least a Champions League place. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Norwich City at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Arsenal at the Vitality Stadium, Chelsea v Southampton at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v West Ham United at Selhurst Park, Everton v Burnley at Goodison Park, Manchester United v Newcastle United at Old Trafford, and Sheffield United v Watford at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Young Viking Hiccup has successfully persuaded his people to live in peace with dragons, but now faces a new threat from a warlord who is capturing the flying lizards to build an army. As Hiccup tries to keep the peace, he is reunited with his long-lost mother. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Jay Baruchel, Gerard Butler and Cate Blanchett(n)

programme.title: Book of Dragons (2011)
programme.desc: Animated short based on the 2010 fantasy film How to Train Your Dragon. Hiccup, Astrid, Fishlegs, Toothless and Gobber uncover the legend behind the Book of Dragons and reveal insider training secrets regarding some new, never-before-seen mythical creatures. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Craig Ferguson(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: A compilation of some of the most spectacular footage from the series, capturing wildlife from around the world on film using the latest camera technology, and revealing how they adapt to overcome the challenges posed by living in jungles, mountains, deserts, islands, grasslands and cities(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark (1981)
programme.desc: Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones goes in search of the legendary Ark of the Covenant, a biblical artefact of devastating power. His quest leads him to Egypt, where the Nazis are plotting to use the Ark to further Hitler's plans for world domination - aided by Jones's deceitful French rival. Steven Spielberg's action adventure, with Harrison Ford, Karen Allen, Paul Freeman and John Rhys-Davies(n)

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba(n)

programme.title: Grandpa's Great Escape
programme.desc: Jack's grandfather was a Second World War flying ace who now sadly has Alzheimer's disease, and when his family can no longer look after him he is moved to Twilight Towers care home, run by matron Miss Dandy. It soon becomes clear she is running the place for her own ulterior motives and it is up to Jack to help his grandpa make a daring escape and once again take to the skies in a Spitfire. Adaptation of David Walliams' children's book, starring Tom Courtenay, Kit Connor and Jennifer Saunders(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers a host of people including pop stars, comedians, sports stars and presenters to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: Second of two family dramas about a walking, talking scarecrow. The Green Man, who creates scarecrows and is the keeper of scarecrow lore, arrives at the farm - and is not at all happy that Worzel is consorting with humans. Meanwhile, Susan and George go to visit Lady Bloomsbury Barton, who is holding a fete with a scarecrow competition that Worzel is determined to win. What will his most competitive rival, Soggy Bogart, make of it all? Mackenzie Crook and Michael Palin star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen puts her foot in it by telling Gray that Chantelle was stressed about the wedding vow renewal, so he decides to take matters into his own hands and set up a makeshift ceremony at home. How will his wife react? Meanwhile, Patrick ignores Denise and Jack in the cafe, so Lola offers him some advice(n)

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Documentary looking at how the quiz show has evolved since it was first shown in January 1970 to remain successful in the present day. Archive footage includes when 19 million people tuned in to see the Princess Royal line-up alongside captain Emlyn Hughes, and guests relive their memories of appearing alongside past hosts David Vine and David Coleman, as well as current incumbent Sue Barker. Among the many contributors are Sebastian Coe, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Alan Shearer, Denise Lewis, Gary Lineker, Gaby Logan and current captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson(n)

programme.title: Welsh Sports Review 2019
programme.desc: Nathan Blake presents a look back at a memorable year in Welsh sport, featuring the national football team's successful Euro 2020 qualification campaign and the rugby union side's Grand Slam triumph and run to the World Cup semi-finals in Warren Gatland's final year as coach. Interviewees include Gatland, world champion boxer Lauren Price and Paralympic skier Menna Fitzpatrick(n)

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the history of Welsh comedy on radio, film and TV output, and looks back to a time when millions tuned in to a wartime radio show that was a platform for countless Welsh stars. He discusses comedy films that defined their eras, as well as much-loved sitcoms and sketch shows with their creators and stars.(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: A compilation of the popular news looking back at the big news of 2019, a year that was largely dominated by Brexit. Eventually a General Election was held - and the rest is history. Mind you, there was some joy courtesy of the England cricket team, with their memorable victory over New Zealand in the World Cup, even if it was by the slightest of margins. Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs in highlights of the year's shows(n)

programme.title: Ant-Man (2015)
programme.desc: A thief attempting to go straight is recruited by a scientist who provides him with a suit that gives him the power to shrink. He must use his new skills to carry out a special mission - helping the inventor to steal back his miniaturisation technology from a ruthless businessman who intends to turn it into a deadly weapon. Superhero adventure, starring Paul Rudd, Michael Douglas and Evangeline Lilly(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Paul A Young and Tommy Banks, plus special guest Emilia Fox, while drinks expert Helen McGinn selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker reviews the Boxing Day fixtures, along with last night's Premier League game between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Manchester City, and looks ahead to Arsenal v Chelsea(n)

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom (1984)
programme.desc: The intrepid archaeologist, with his young sidekick and a nightclub singer in tow, is stranded in India, where he takes on a mission to recover magical sacred stones from the hands of an evil cult engaged in human sacrifice and child slavery. Steven Spielberg's action adventure, starring Harrison Ford, Kate Capshaw, Ke Huy Quan and Amrish Puri(n)

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. Led by the eternally upbeat Joy, they have always ensured she lived a happy life - but when Riley's parents move to a new city, she is plunged into depression and the emotions face a crisis that threatens their entire world. Pixar animated fantasy, with the voices of Amy Poehler and Phyllis Smith(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers a host of people including pop stars, comedians, sports stars and presenters to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Alice Through the Looking Glass (2016)
programme.desc: Alice Kingsleigh has spent three years sailing the high seas. Upon her return to London from China, she returns to the magical world of Underland and takes a journey back in time to save the Mad Hatter and her friends. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Johnny Depp and Anne Hathaway(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A Christmas edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four famous teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. Featuring Christopher Biggins, Paul Zerdin, Gary Wilmot, Michelle Collins, Debbie McGee, Richard Cadell with Sooty, Faye Tozer and Duncan James. With a special performance from Aled Jones and Russell Watson. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: An emotional rollercoaster of a day is in store for Rash, with events taking their toll on staff and patients alike. Due to constant tension between various members of the team, Dylan finds it difficult to keep control of the department, and an inspection by a member of the management team does little to ease his frustration(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League games, including West Ham United v Leicester City at London Stadium and Norwich City v Tottenham Hotspur at Carrow Road. Both fixtures between the Hammers and Foxes ended in draws last season, with the corresponding match on this ground finishing 2-2. There are also highlights of Brighton & Hove Albion v Bournemouth at the Amex Stadium, Burnley v Manchester United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Everton at St James' Park, Southampton v Crystal Palace at St Mary's Stadium, Watford v Aston Villa at Vicarage Road, and Friday night's clash between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Manchester City at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Screen Grab
programme.desc: Presenter Claire Lim and her team of experts help the Mitchell family in Ayrshire confront their obsession with screens, and reduce the amount of time they spend in front of them. They introduce the family to their Screen Grab kit - sophisticated technology to show them just how they have become so badly disconnected in an attempt to make their change their habits(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

The Green Man, who creates scarecrows and is the keeper of scarecrow lore, arrives at the farm - and is not at all happy that Worzel is consorting with humans. Meanwhile, Susan and George go to visit Lady Bloomsbury Barton, who is holding a fete with a scarecrow competition that Worzel is determined to win. What will his most competitive rival, Soggy Bogart, make of it all? Mackenzie Crook and Michael Palin star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen puts her foot in it by telling Gray that Chantelle was stressed about the wedding vow renewal, so he decides to take matters into his own hands and set up a makeshift ceremony at home. How will his wife react? Meanwhile, Patrick ignores Denise and Jack in the cafe, so Lola offers him some advice(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Kim is determined to give Isobel the perfect Christmas Day with all the O'Haras, while the McLeans' celebrations are hijacked by an unwanted visitor Elsewhere, Lenny and Alex bond over the Muppets, and Eve tries to cheer up a lonely Lou(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: A compilation of the popular news looking back at the big news of 2019, a year that was largely dominated by Brexit. Eventually a General Election was held - and the rest is history. Mind you, there was some joy courtesy of the England cricket team, with their memorable victory over New Zealand in the World Cup, even if it was by the slightest of margins. Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs in highlights of the year's shows(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Christmas is supposed to be a time for family and friends, so when it seems that this year Mrs Brown cannot please anyone, she wonders if it would have been better had she never been born. Enter Clyde, a mysterious figure claiming to be an angel, who tells the doting mother he can show her what life would have been like without her - and before she knows it, she finds herself in a world she doesn't recognise, and that doesn't recognise her. Brendan O'Carroll stars in this spoof of classic Christmas movie It's a Wonderful Life, with former Corrie star Kevin Kennedy (Curly Watts)(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Documentary looking at how the quiz show has evolved since it was first shown in January 1970 to remain successful in the present day. Archive footage includes when 19 million people tuned in to see the Princess Royal line-up alongside captain Emlyn Hughes, and guests relive their memories of appearing alongside past hosts David Vine and David Coleman, as well as current incumbent Sue Barker. Among the many contributors are Sebastian Coe, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Alan Shearer, Denise Lewis, Gary Lineker, Gaby Logan and current captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the Boxing Day Premiership fixtures, including Hearts v Hibernian, St Mirren v Celtic and Rangers v Kilmarnock(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire has a crisis and turns to Fleabag for help, while a familiar face makes a reappearance at the cafe. Hilary makes a friend, Martin demands answers, and Godmother and Dad's wedding hangs in the balance. Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott, Sian Clifford, Bill Patterson, Olivia Colman, Hugh Dennis and Katy Wix(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Paul A Young and Tommy Banks, plus special guest Emilia Fox, while drinks expert Helen McGinn selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Ron, Linzey, Linsay and Kay - colleagues from the Cairn O'Mohr Winery in Perth - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker reviews the Boxing Day fixtures, along with last night's Premier League game between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Manchester City, and looks ahead to Arsenal v Chelsea(n)

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom (1984)
programme.desc: The intrepid archaeologist, with his young sidekick and a nightclub singer in tow, is stranded in India, where he takes on a mission to recover magical sacred stones from the hands of an evil cult engaged in human sacrifice and child slavery. Steven Spielberg's action adventure, starring Harrison Ford, Kate Capshaw, Ke Huy Quan and Amrish Puri(n)

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. Led by the eternally upbeat Joy, they have always ensured she lived a happy life - but when Riley's parents move to a new city, she is plunged into depression and the emotions face a crisis that threatens their entire world. Pixar animated fantasy, with the voices of Amy Poehler and Phyllis Smith(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers a host of people including pop stars, comedians, sports stars and presenters to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Alice Through the Looking Glass (2016)
programme.desc: Alice Kingsleigh has spent three years sailing the high seas. Upon her return to London from China, she returns to the magical world of Underland and takes a journey back in time to save the Mad Hatter and her friends. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Johnny Depp and Anne Hathaway(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A Christmas edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four famous teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. Featuring Christopher Biggins, Paul Zerdin, Gary Wilmot, Michelle Collins, Debbie McGee, Richard Cadell with Sooty, Faye Tozer and Duncan James. With a special performance from Aled Jones and Russell Watson. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: An emotional rollercoaster of a day is in store for Rash, with events taking their toll on staff and patients alike. Due to constant tension between various members of the team, Dylan finds it difficult to keep control of the department, and an inspection by a member of the management team does little to ease his frustration(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League games, including West Ham United v Leicester City at London Stadium and Norwich City v Tottenham Hotspur at Carrow Road. Both fixtures between the Hammers and Foxes ended in draws last season, with the corresponding match on this ground finishing 2-2. There are also highlights of Brighton & Hove Albion v Bournemouth at the Amex Stadium, Burnley v Manchester United at Turf Moor, Newcastle United v Everton at St James' Park, Southampton v Crystal Palace at St Mary's Stadium, Watford v Aston Villa at Vicarage Road, and Friday night's clash between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Manchester City at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: The Good Dinosaur (2015)
programme.desc: In a world where the asteroid impact that wiped out the dinosaurs never happened, humans have evolved as a primitive species while the giant lizards remain the dominant life form on Earth. A young apatosaurus becomes separated from his family, but finds a friend in a feral human boy as he makes the dangerous journey home. Pixar animated adventure, with the voices of Raymond Ochoa and Jack Bright(n)

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Snow
programme.desc: Undercover cameras reveal how living in the snow tests animals to their limit. In New Zealand, cameras follow a group of kea parrots, revealing how friendship is at the heart of the lives of these sociable and highly intelligent birds. In the North Pole, cameras get up close to play-fighting polar bears, while on the other side of the planet at the South Pole, footage reveals the parental devotion that emperor penguins have as they try to secure the survival of their offspring and face some of the harshest conditions on Earth. In Tasmania, the spy wallaby finds love - discovering along the way an interesting courtship technique among wombats(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Room on the Broom
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It tells the story of a kind witch who invites a surprising number of animals to ride on her broom - much to the frustration of her cat. But when a fearsome dragon appears, what will the gang do? Gillian Anderson heads the voice cast as the witch, with Rob Brydon, Martin Clunes, Sally Hawkins and David Walliams as the animals, Timothy Spall as the dragon and Simon Pegg as narrator(n)

programme.title: The Boss Baby (2017)
programme.desc: A youngster believes his baby brother is actually a power-hungry tycoon sent to destroy his perfect family life. However, the duo must put aside their differences and work together when they uncover a plot to sabotage the company their parents work for. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Alec Baldwin, Steve Buscemi, Jimmy Kimmel and Lisa Kudrow(n)

programme.title: Mimi and the Mountain Dragon
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Michael Morpurgo's children's book about a brave but shy girl from a village that lives in constant fear of a terrible mountain dragon. On a snowy winter's night, she finds a baby dragon asleep in her family's woodshed, and sets out on a terrible journey through a blizzard to return the infant to its dreaded mother. Featuring an introduction by the author and a score by Oscar-winning composer Rachel Portman, performed by the BBC Philharmonic(n)

programme.title: The Jungle Book (2016)
programme.desc: A boy is raised by animals in the jungles of India, but his carefree life is threatened by the return of a fierce tiger with a grudge against humans. The youngster faces a difficult choice between remaining with his friends or returning to his own people. Live action remake of Disney's animated adventure based on Rudyard Kipling's stories, starring Neel Sethi, with the voices of Bill Murray, Ben Kingsley and Idris Elba(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: City children Susan and John are spending their summer at Scatterbrook Farm, where they are amazed to discover the scarecrow of Ten Acre Field can come to life. But it seems this extraordinary new friendship has thrown the rhythm of the natural world out of kilter and now they must work to put things right. Mackenzie Crook writes, directs and stars in the first of two new dramas based on the books by Barbara Euphan Todd - last adapted 40 years ago in the popular ITV series with Jon Pertwee. Co-starring Steve Pemberton, Rosie Cavaliero and Vicki Pepperdine(n)

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with the voices of Ben Whishaw and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The events of Christmas Day weigh heavy in Walford, with more than one family left ripped apart. Sharon and Keanu's worlds have been shattered - but how far has Phil gone? On a happier note, Gray and Chantelle are excited about renewing their wedding vows but their plans are ruined when the venue cancels. Patrick is unhappy about Denise meddling in his personal life and takes an extreme course of action(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure victory. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the Christmas fun are Liz Bonnin, Stephen Merchant, Sharon Osbourne and Ranj Singh(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker introduces highlights of the Boxing Day Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool at the King Power Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Foxes and Reds had both made excellent starts to the season, with Brendan Rodgers' side emerging as surprise contenders for the title - or at the very least a Champions League place. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Norwich City at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Arsenal at the Vitality Stadium, Chelsea v Southampton at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v West Ham United at Selhurst Park, Everton v Burnley at Goodison Park, Manchester United v Newcastle United at Old Trafford, and Sheffield United v Watford at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The comedy star brings the latest series of his entertainment showcase to a conclusion with his annual festive special from the London Palladium. Tonight, global superstar Sharon Osbourne hands over her phone to play Send to All?, and Strictly Come Dancing judge Craig Revel Horwood is woken up by Michael in the middle of the night for the Midnight Gameshow. Plus, another Unexpected Star puts on a spectacular festive performance(n)

programme.title: Take Off with Bradley and Holly
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh and Holly Willoughby present a special Christmas family entertainment show, which offers members of the audience the chance to win the holiday of a lifetime to Lapland. The show will also feature special games, surprises and appearances by celebrity guests(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker introduces highlights of the Boxing Day Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool at the King Power Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Foxes and Reds had both made excellent starts to the season, with Brendan Rodgers' side emerging as surprise contenders for the title - or at the very least a Champions League place. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Norwich City at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Arsenal at the Vitality Stadium, Chelsea v Southampton at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v West Ham United at Selhurst Park, Everton v Burnley at Goodison Park, Manchester United v Newcastle United at Old Trafford, and Sheffield United v Watford at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Young Viking Hiccup has successfully persuaded his people to live in peace with dragons, but now faces a new threat from a warlord who is capturing the flying lizards to build an army. As Hiccup tries to keep the peace, he is reunited with his long-lost mother. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Jay Baruchel, Gerard Butler and Cate Blanchett(n)

programme.title: Book of Dragons (2011)
programme.desc: Animated short based on the 2010 fantasy film How to Train Your Dragon. Hiccup, Astrid, Fishlegs, Toothless and Gobber uncover the legend behind the Book of Dragons and reveal insider training secrets regarding some new, never-before-seen mythical creatures. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Craig Ferguson(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: A compilation of some of the most spectacular footage from the series, capturing wildlife from around the world on film using the latest camera technology, and revealing how they adapt to overcome the challenges posed by living in jungles, mountains, deserts, islands, grasslands and cities(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark (1981)
programme.desc: Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones goes in search of the legendary Ark of the Covenant, a biblical artefact of devastating power. His quest leads him to Egypt, where the Nazis are plotting to use the Ark to further Hitler's plans for world domination - aided by Jones's deceitful French rival. Steven Spielberg's action adventure, with Harrison Ford, Karen Allen, Paul Freeman and John Rhys-Davies(n)

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba(n)

programme.title: Grandpa's Great Escape
programme.desc: Jack's grandfather was a Second World War flying ace who now sadly has Alzheimer's disease, and when his family can no longer look after him he is moved to Twilight Towers care home, run by matron Miss Dandy. It soon becomes clear she is running the place for her own ulterior motives and it is up to Jack to help his grandpa make a daring escape and once again take to the skies in a Spitfire. Adaptation of David Walliams' children's book, starring Tom Courtenay, Kit Connor and Jennifer Saunders(n)

programme.title: Give My Head Peace
programme.desc: Uncle Andy invites a dying Catholic Priest back to his house to spend his last Christmas. Has Andy finally changed his sectarian ways? Award-winning comedy, starring Tim McGarry, Olivia Nash and Marty Reid(n)

programme.title: Ant-Man (2015)
programme.desc: A thief attempting to go straight is recruited by a scientist who provides him with a suit that gives him the power to shrink. He must use his new skills to carry out a special mission - helping the inventor to steal back his miniaturisation technology from a ruthless businessman who intends to turn it into a deadly weapon. Superhero adventure, starring Paul Rudd, Michael Douglas and Evangeline Lilly(n)

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to Ian Murray about his new book 'This is Our Story', on the fight to save Heart of Midlothian F.C. from liquidation(n)

programme.title: Reflections: When Parties Split
programme.desc: In the third programme of unscripted talks on party splits, Steve Richards looks at Ramsey Macdonald and the Labour party split on formation of the National Government(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Scottish First Minister making a speech in Edinburgh calling on the UK government to negotiate a transfer of powers to Holyrood to allow another referendum on Scottish independence, from Thursday 19 December(n)

programme.title: Thursday in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of Thursday in Parliament presented by Alicia McCarthy(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to professor Ruth Kinna about her new book 'The Government of No One - The Theory and Practice of Anarchism(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues that judges - especially those of the European Court of Human Rights - have usurped power by expanding the interpretation of human rights law. He explains that concepts of human rights have a long history in the common law. But by contrast, the European Convention on Human Rights has become a dynamic treaty, taking on new interpretations and powers(n)

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the House of Lords of a peer thanking the Queen for delivering the Queen's Speech in parliament, from Thursday 19 December(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Yeoman Warder Christopher Skaife talks to Mark D'Arcy about his new book 'The Ravenmaster' on life in the Tower of London.(n)

programme.title: Reflections: When Parties Split
programme.desc: Series of half-hour unscripted talks on party splits(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: The review of the day's events in Westminster(n)

programme.title: Mayor's Question Time
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the London Assembly of questions to the Mayor of London, Sadiq Khan(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to historian Lauren Johnson about her new book Shadow King: The Life and Death of Henry VI(n)

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: BBC Wales journalist Elliw Gwawr visits Highgate Cottage in Llanystumdwy, the childhood home of First World War Premier David Lloyd George(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: The speech and question session with former prime minister Tony Blair on the future for Labour and progressive politics, from Wednesday 18 December(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to former supreme court justice Jonathan Sumption about his new book 'Trials of the State: Law and the Decline of Politics'(n)

programme.title: Reflections: When Parties Split
programme.desc: In the final programme in a series of half-hour unscripted talks on party splits, political journalist Steve Richards looks at how Brexit has split the two main political parties(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: A panel of scholars debate the legacy of the Treaty of Versailles at an event at the LSE, with professors Michael Cox, David Stevenson, Linda Yueh and Barry Buzan, from Thursday 28 November.(n)

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to politics professors Tim Bale and Paul Webb about their new book 'Footsoldiers' on political party membership.(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues against Britain adopting a written constitution as a response to political alienation, suggesting that it would be better change the system of voting. Presented and chaired by Anita Anand(n)

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Party historian Lord Lexden visits Astley Hall, home to the Conservative leader who was three times Prime Minister(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: He is one of the most controversial figures to have sat in the Speaker's chair in the House of Commons. As John Bercow bows out after ten controversial years as Speaker, BBC Parliament revisits the career of a modernising but polarising figure. See the teenage Bercow's ‘Question Time' debut, not from the Commons but on the TV programme. Relive the former Tory MP's clashes with ministers as he's accused of bias and of bending the rules to try to stop Brexit. In addition - with more Commons archive - Bercow Bows Out offers the chance to re-live some of his favourite unique phrases, whether he's advising an MP to search for ‘a soothing medicament' or accusing others of ‘chuntering from a sedentary position'(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: D-Day 75
programme.desc: Three hundred veterans, aged between 90 and 105, board a ship to return to the D-Day beaches in Normandy. They were young men in 1944 when they took part in the largest seaborne landings ever mounted. Some are returning for the first time, some for the last and they will remember the thousands who never came home. One veteran, Jack Mortimer, now 95, says he simply wants to do his duty and raise a salute to the real heroes, while other featured veterans include Ernie Pegg, who drove supply lorries, Ken Peppercorn, who was wounded and captured after the landings, Eric Strange, who commanded one of the landing craft, and Ken Tanner, who landed with his tank crew(n)

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: For 4 months protests have rocked Hong Kong, pitting hundreds of thousands of young, idealistic demonstrators against the authorities and the might of China. The clashes between police and protestors have become increasingly violent and neither side shows signs of backing down. Our World goes beyond the frontline to tell the stories of some of those involved, what drives them - and their hopes and fears for the future(n)

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: For 4 months protests have rocked Hong Kong, pitting hundreds of thousands of young, idealistic demonstrators against the authorities and the might of China. The clashes between police and protestors have become increasingly violent and neither side shows signs of backing down. Our World goes beyond the frontline to tell the stories of some of those involved, what drives them - and their hopes and fears for the future(n)

programme.title: D-Day 75
programme.desc: Three hundred veterans, aged between 90 and 105, board a ship to return to the D-Day beaches in Normandy. They were young men in 1944 when they took part in the largest seaborne landings ever mounted. Some are returning for the first time, some for the last and they will remember the thousands who never came home. One veteran, Jack Mortimer, now 95, says he simply wants to do his duty and raise a salute to the real heroes, while other featured veterans include Ernie Pegg, who drove supply lorries, Ken Peppercorn, who was wounded and captured after the landings, Eric Strange, who commanded one of the landing craft, and Ken Tanner, who landed with his tank crew(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's in a triathlon and takes Fizz's superhuman strength headband, which Fizz needs to beat the President at a thumb war(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Today's the big day, and Magoo believes he is in a recording studio to make a hit single for famous producer, Phil Dustspeck. But is that really true?(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Toby Jones and Phyllis Logan(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill revisits some of the nation's favourite funniest videos, using his unique narration to turn viewers' slapstick into celebrity satire - but who will earn a place in the hall of fame?(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 3.05 Ladbrokes King George VI Steeple Chase from Kempton Park, which follows races at 1.20, 1.55 and 2.30. Plus, a race from 2.10 at Wetherby. With analysis from Ruby Walsh and AP McCoy, reports by Mick Fitzgerald, Luke Harvey, Matt Chapman and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Tenable All Stars
programme.desc: Warwick Davis presents a Christmas-themed version of the Top 10 general knowledge quiz. Joining him are a star-studded team of panto celebrities - Debbie McGee, Louie Spence, Paul Chuckle, Rustie Lee and Paul Zerdin - who'll be taking on the Tenable Tower for the chance to win £125,000 for their chosen charities(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a festive special of the game show from Manchester, where he sets members of the public unusual Christmas-themed challenges and unwraps a variety of hilarious games(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian helps out at Battersea Dogs & Cats Home over the festive period, where he is enlisted to help pooches with behavioural problems. They include a reclusive mongrel that refuses to leave its kennel and a grumpy Yorkshire terrier that refuses to walk. Paul also helps an overprotective staffie mum struggling to say goodbye to her pups and an excitable one-year-old puppy that just cannot stop having little accidents. He also gets out and about with the street vets to bring festive joy to London's homeless and their dogs(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham pleads his case to Rhona, David is determined to keep things quiet, and Nicola reaches out to Laurel(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Following one of the most dramatic Christmas Days in Weatherfield history, the long-suffering Street residents come to terms with the aftermath and try to get their lives back in order(n)

programme.title: Skyfall (2012)
programme.desc: James Bond pursues a mysterious criminal mastermind who has orchestrated a terrorist attack on MI6 headquarters and exposed the identities of undercover agents around the world. The trail leads to a rogue former operative who has a personal vendetta against the intelligence organisation's leader M - who is also facing calls to retire. Sam Mendes directs the 23rd instalment in the spy franchise, starring Daniel Craig as 007, with Javier Bardem, Judi Dench, Ralph Fiennes, Naomie Harris and Ben Whishaw(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes another batch of celebrity guests to his famous green room for a special Christmas edition of the talk show. He is joined by David Tennant, whose demonic role in Terry Pratchett and Neil Gaiman's Good Omens will be coming to BBC in the new year, as well as singers Rita Ora and Jamie Cullum, former X Factor judge Sharon Osbourne, Strictly Come Dancing: It Takes Two presenter Rylan Clark-Neal, award-winning Hollywood star David Oyelowo and comedian Bec Hill(n)

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: TV presenter Charlotte Hawkins puts on a dinner party for four strangers, who have to work out the identity of their mystery celebrity host with the help of a few cryptic clues - otherwise they will be doing the washing up. Hosted by Brian McFadden(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott continues his tour of Jamaica, taking a trip into the Blue Mountains to learn the secrets of the region's famous coffee. He also visits one of the island's most acclaimed jerk chicken establishments and tries to get his hands on their secret recipe, before whipping up a mango and pineapple milkshake at famous hotel Jamaica Inn(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: The Mummy: Tomb of the Dragon Emperor (2008)
programme.desc: Daredevil explorers Rick and Evelyn O'Connell are called into action when their son accidentally resurrects the evil Chinese Emperor Han from his ancient tomb. The family now faces a race against time to prevent the merciless leader from awakening his vast terracotta army in a plot to take over the world. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Brendan Fraser, Maria Bello, Jet Li and Michelle Yeoh(n)

programme.title: Jack Frost (1998)
programme.desc: A rock musician who realises he is spending too much time away from his family is killed in a car accident while travelling home for the festive season. The following year his spirit takes on the form of a snowman, made by his son, allowing them to spend one last Christmas together. Fantasy drama, starring Michael Keaton, Kelly Preston, Joseph Cross and Mark Addy(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five wheelchair basketball enthusiasts answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts a special seasonal edition of the quiz, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine in the hope of winning £20,000 for charity. Donning panto outfits to get into the festive spirit - oh yes they do - Lesley Joseph, Vicky Pattison and Christopher Biggins all bid to land their charities a Christmas gift to remember(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Coronation Street stars Simon Gregson, Victoria Ekanoye, Lucy Fallon and Andrew Whyment pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim tries to get Jamie on side, Rhona worries what people might think, and David's anxiety grows(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Michelle heads to the bistro to hand over the keys to the new owner, but when the mystery buyer turns out to be Ray, she sets about smashing up the place. Elsewhere, Ali reveals that he's told the police about Gary's secret life as a loan shark, and a mortified Fiz confesses to Tyrone and Jade how she bought a gun for the shop, and it was then stolen(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's a time of joy and happiness in Midsomer for once - Christmas is coming, and DCI Barnaby and his wife Sarah are soon to become parents. But the festive mood changes when a man is stabbed to death with an antique sword during a ghost-hunting party at a spooky manor house in Morton Shallows. programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald(n)

However, he cannot afford to anger the king of France, and tries to make it look as if his queen has had an affair. Comedy, starring Sid James, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Terry Scott, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor and Kenneth Connor(n)

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott(n)

programme.title: Carry On Behind (1975)
programme.desc: Professors Ronald Crump and Anna Vooshka embark on an archaeological dig, but find their efforts disrupted by the saucy shenanigans of a motley bunch of holiday-makers in the neighbouring caravan park. Comedy, starring Kenneth Williams, Elke Sommer, Joan Sims, Windsor Davies, Kenneth Connor and Bernard Bresslaw(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Jungle (1970)
programme.desc: A Victorian explorer recalls how he was recruited by an aristocrat to find her missing son in the African jungle. Teaming up with a fearless hunter, he hoped to find a rare bird on his journey - but instead fell into the clutches of an all-female tribe. Comedy, starring Sid James, Frankie Howerd, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Terry Scott(n)

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques(n)

programme.title: Carry On Follow That Camel (1967)
programme.desc: A disgraced English gentleman joins the Foreign Legion to restore his honour, and proves himself an unlikely hero. Meanwhile, his naive lost love learns he was innocent, and heads to the desert to bring him home. Comedy, starring Phil Silvers, Jim Dale, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Peter Butterworth, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When dock worker Owen Thorne plunges from the roof of a multi-storey car park while picking up his daughter from her school prom, Vera immediately detects foul play. The team soon discovers a work dispute over the death of a young man, with everyone believing Owen was to blame, and the victim's father admits he wanted revenge - but his alibi holds. The mystery then continues when Vera spots an inconsistency in the statement Owen's wife gave to the police, suggesting she's not certain of her husband's fidelity(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when the body of a wildlife ranger is found on a remote island off the coast of Northumberland. Her death is at first assumed to be an accident, since she was alone on the island when she died, but marks on the body seem to point to murder. As Vera delves into the dead woman's personal life, she uncovers dark secrets and old resentments(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Timid new student nurse Faye Clark is faced with a trying first day when a model collapses after taking magic mushrooms. Lizzie falls for an injured motorcycle stuntman who is convinced he is destined to live fast and die young due to a hereditary condition - but Ormerod comes up with a different diagnosis. Lauren Drummond, Michelle Hardwick, James Daffern and Robert Daws star(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' bed collapses and George finds himself short of cash to acquire a new one. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: An old flame of Mildred's turns up at the Roper house, prompting George to blow the cobwebs off his little black book. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey(n)

programme.title: Goodbye Mr Chips
programme.desc: Martin Clunes stars as an earnest Latin master working at the Brookfield School of the 1880s, where he meets and marries his true love Kathie and finds himself touched by politics, grief and the utter devastation of World War One. Charting more than 50 years of one man's extraordinary life, the story ends in the 1930s, as a wise, elderly and much-loved Mr Chipping lives out his last days in retirement. Frank Delaney's adaptation of James Hilton's timeless classic, which has never been out of print since first appearing as a magazine serialisation in 1934, and for which Robert Donat whipped an Oscar from under the nose of Gone with the Wind's Clark Gable in the 1939 film production. Also starring Victoria Hamilton, Conleth Hill and Patrick Malahide(n)

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
programme.desc: Youngster Dorothy Gale is whisked over the rainbow by a tornado into the magical land of Oz, where she inadvertently kills a wicked witch - incurring the wrath of another. Setting out to find the wizard who can show her a way home, she is joined on her journey by a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who all hope their own wishes will be granted. Musical fantasy, with Judy Garland, Ray Bolger, Jack Haley, Bert Lahr and Frank Morgan(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp (1980)
programme.desc: Seedy landlord Rigsby takes in a seemingly respectable new lodger who jeopardises his efforts to romance tenant Miss Jones - but the new arrival may not be all he appears. Meanwhile, the addition of a rugby playing art student to the house sparks a fitness craze that leads Rigsby to enter a boxing match. Comedy based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: Willy Wonka and the Chocolate Factory (1971)
programme.desc: An eccentric confectionery king announces that five children will be given a tour of his wondrous factory. The winners turn out to be a kind-hearted boy from a penniless family and four obnoxious brats, who face a terrible fate when they misbehave. Musical fantasy, based on Roald Dahl's classic book Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, starring Gene Wilder, Jack Albertson, Peter Ostrum and Roy Kinnear(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Les Dawson Forever
programme.desc: The first of two programmes celebrating the career of the Manchester-born comedy star. Les's youngest daughter Charlotte Dawson meets Opportunity Knocks director Royston Mayoh to hear how an audition would change her father's life for ever, Les's comedy partner Roy Barraclough reminisces about their famous characters Cissie and Ada, and Barry Cryer returns to Yorkshire Television to revisit some of Les's friends(n)

programme.title: Les Dawson Forever
programme.desc: Part two of two. Members of the dance troupe the Roly Polys reunite on stage to reminisce about working alongside Les and Les's wife Tracy Dawson reunites with karaoke superfan Penny Morgan, who was given the opportunity to perform the duet of a lifetime on Cilla Black's Surprise Surprise in 1993(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into Newcastle's underworld to discover the identity of a murder victim found dead in the river, with no clue as to who he is apart from a distinctive drugs wrapper. She discovers the dead man had ties to some very dangerous people, many of whom may have wanted him dead, while a witness's testimony links the case to a remote farm where a murder took place 13 years previously. The connection brings Kenny's past back to haunt him, as he was responsible for seeing the perpetrator convicted(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A cancer patient dies unexpectedly after a visit from Dr Ormerod and the grieving family points the finger at him - leading to his arrest on suspicion of murder. Hospital drama, starring Robert Daws, Neil McDermott, Lauren Drummond and Gareth Hale(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan decides to boost his income and applies for a post as a ticket inspector. Vintage Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney, Cicely Courtneidge and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is lumbered with the job of running the depot canteen, and makes the mistake of appointing Olive to do the cooking. With her in charge of the kitchen, disaster figures prominently on the menu. Vintage Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses (1971)
programme.desc: Wily bus driver Stan Butler thinks a new company policy that allows women behind the wheel will rob him of his precious overtime money. programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Continuing the final series of the hospital drama after a two-year break. Susie Dixon's head is turned by a rich playboy, with devastating consequences for Mrs Middleditch when the negligent nurse administers the wrong blood during a transfusion. Ormerod is called out by the coastguard to rescue a grieving widow who has got into difficulty on her yacht, and Jack and Alun find themselves looking after a horse after it tramples its owner. Robert Daws, Sarah Beck Mather, Susan Hampshire and Gareth Hale star(n)
